title: Emergency Field Delivery
immediate_steps: Ensure a clean environment, wash hands, and prepare clean towels
Do not pull the baby; let the mother push
Support the baby's head as it emerges, do not pull on the neck
Once born, wipe fluid from the baby's mouth and nose to clear airways
Keep the baby warm by placing it skin-to-skin on the mother's chest
Wait for the placenta to deliver naturally (usually within 20 mins); do not pull the umbilical cord
Tie the umbilical cord tightly in two places (about 4 and 6 inches from the baby) with sterile string, then cut between the ties with a sterilized blade
avoid: Do not let the mother go to the bathroom if delivery is imminent
Do not slap the baby's back to make it cry (rubbing the back gently is enough)
